schools.summary: Gill: it was a year after, in wheat harvest; her father would not suffer him to go in.

themes: Samson visited his wife with a kid... But her father would not suffer him to go in
readiness for peace met with rejection by the unfaithful
I am for peace: but when I speak, they are for war (Ps. 120:7)

schools.summary: Gill: he affirmed confidently he thought Samson utterly hated her; offered the younger sister instead.

themes: I verily thought that thou hadst utterly hated her... is not her younger sister fairer than she? take her
the world making light of faithfulness, offering a substitute for its wrong
they speak vanity every one with his neighbour: with flattering lips... they speak (Ps. 12:2)

schools.summary: Gill: he said this respecting them, either within himself or openly.
Keil & Delitzsch: his words, now shall I be blameless.

themes: Now shall I be more blameless than the Philistines, though I do them a displeasure
the appointed judge against the oppressors, yet vengeance belonging to God
avenge not yourselves... Vengeance is mine; I will repay, saith the Lord (Rom. 12:19)

schools.summary: Gill: this should not be thought incredible, since Canaan abounded with foxes; he turned tail to tail with a firebrand between.

themes: Samson... caught three hundred foxes, and took firebrands, and... put a firebrand in the midst between two tails
the resourcefulness God gives his instruments to smite the enemy
whatsoever the LORD pleased, that did he in heaven, and in earth (Ps. 135:6)

schools.summary: Gill: the foxes, frightened by the fire near them, ran into the standing corn; the shocks, standing corn, vineyards, and olives were burnt.

themes: he let them go into the standing corn of the Philistines, and burnt up both the shocks, and also the standing corn, with the vineyards and olives
God stripping the oppressor of his wealth in a moment
the increase of his house shall depart... in the day of his wrath (Job 20:28)

schools.summary: Gill: they inquired who was the author of the mischief.
Clarke: they burnt her and her father, the very thing first threatened.

themes: the Philistines came up, and burnt her and her father with fire
the very fire she feared and betrayed him to escape now consuming her
the fear of the wicked, it shall come upon him (Prov. 10:24)

schools.summary: Gill: they had burnt his wife and her father, thinking to appease him.
Keil & Delitzsch: his word, yet will I be avenged.

themes: Though ye have done this, yet will I be avenged of you, and after that I will cease
the bitter, endless cycle of vengeance one wrong breeds in another
recompense to no man evil for evil... avenge not yourselves (Rom. 12:17-19)

schools.summary: Gill: he smote them on hip and thigh with his hands, having no weapon of war, with a great slaughter; dwelt in the top of the rock Etam.

themes: he smote them hip and thigh with a great slaughter: and he went down and dwelt in the top of the rock Etam
God making his servant mighty against the foe; the rock a refuge
in the time of trouble... he shall set me up upon a rock (Ps. 27:5)

schools.summary: Gill: they went up from Palestine, which lay low on the seashore.
Keil & Delitzsch: them pitching in Judah and spreading in Lehi.

themes: Then the Philistines went up, and pitched in Judah, and spread themselves in Lehi
Judah sunk in subjection bearing the enemy's invasion for one man's sake
the wicked watcheth the righteous, and seeketh to slay him (Ps. 37:32)

schools.summary: Gill: Judah sent a deputation to know the reason of this formidable appearance.
Keil & Delitzsch: the answer, to bind Samson and do to him as he had done.

themes: To bind Samson are we come up, to do to him as he hath done to us
the enmity of the world against the deliverer God raises up
then... took Jesus, and bound him (John 18:12)

schools.summary: Gill: they went down to the cave of the rock of Etam; said, knowest thou not the Philistines rule over us?

themes: Knowest thou not that the Philistines are rulers over us? what is this that thou hast done unto us?
a people broken by bondage resenting the one who would free them
they answered him, We... were never in bondage to any man (John 8:33)

schools.summary: Gill: they were come down into the cave where he was.
Clarke: the demand that they swear not to fall on him.

themes: We are come down to bind thee, that we may deliver thee into the hand of the Philistines
the betrayal of the deliverer by his own to appease the oppressor
the chief priests... bound Jesus... and delivered him to Pilate (Mark 15:1)

schools.summary: Gill: they would not slay him themselves; nor would the Jews put Christ to death themselves, though his betrayers and murderers; bound him with two new cords.

themes: we will bind thee fast, and deliver thee into their hand: but surely we will not kill thee. And they bound him with two new cords
a shadow of the binding and delivering-up of Christ by his own people
it is not lawful for us to put any man to death (John 18:31)

schools.summary: Gill: Lehi, so called from what happened there, six miles from Etam.
Keil & Delitzsch: the cords became as flax burnt with fire, and his bands loosed.

themes: the Spirit of the LORD came mightily upon him, and the cords... became as flax that was burnt with fire, and his bands loosed
no chains holding the man God empowers; the deliverer cannot be held
it was not possible that he should be holden of it (Acts 2:24)

schools.summary: Gill: the jawbone of an ass lately killed, which perhaps had flesh upon it; he slew a thousand men therewith.

themes: he found a new jawbone of an ass... and slew a thousand men therewith
God working mighty deliverance by the meanest of weapons
God hath chosen the weak things of the world to confound the things which are mighty (1 Cor. 1:27)

schools.summary: Gill: he said this in a kind of triumphant song.
Keil & Delitzsch: the song, heaps upon heaps.

themes: With the jawbone of an ass, heaps upon heaps, with the jaw of an ass have I slain a thousand men
the joy of victory, yet beware ascribing too much to oneself
not unto us, O LORD... but unto thy name give glory (Ps. 115:1)

schools.summary: Gill: the song probably consisted of more than is here expressed.
Ellicott: Ramath-lehi, the hill of the jawbone.

themes: he cast away the jawbone out of his hand, and called that place Ramathlehi
memorials that recall the LORD's deliverances for the generations
this shall be written for the generation to come (Ps. 102:18)

schools.summary: Gill: Josephus thinks the thirst came as a rebuke for ascribing the victory to his own strength; he owned, Thou hast given this great deliverance.

themes: he was sore athirst, and called on the LORD... Thou hast given this great deliverance into the hand of thy servant
God humbling his servant to feel his dependence; the cry of weakness heard
he giveth power to the faint; and to them that have no might he increaseth strength (Isa. 40:29)

schools.summary: Gill: God clave a hollow place, a socket in the form of a mortar, and water came out; he called it En-hakkore, the well of him that cried.

themes: God clave an hollow place... there came water thereout... and he revived: wherefore he called the name thereof Enhakkore
God providing for the fainting and answering the cry of the needy
this poor man cried, and the LORD heard him, and saved him out of all his troubles (Ps. 34:6)

schools.summary: Gill: he only began to deliver Israel, not completely, while the Philistines still had power; he judged Israel twenty years.

themes: And he judged Israel in the days of the Philistines twenty years
the partial deliverers pointing on to the one full and final Deliverer
he shall begin to deliver Israel out of the hand of the Philistines (Judg. 13:5)
